items.title: Why is Apple asking me to pay more for Big Tech’s AI obsession?
items.description:
Tim Cook recently said price increases were "unavoidable" and described the company's pricing as "unsustainable." The 16-inch MacBook Pro saw its price go up by $300. The 11-inch iPad Air went from $599 to $749. Even the HomePod Mini got a $30 bump to $129. Cook squarely placed the blame at the feet of the AI industry, which is not surprising. RAMageddon has already come for your desktop PCs and gaming consoles. The Xbox has seen its price climb nearly 25 percent depending on the model, and Nothing even canceled an entire phone launch. Apple is just the most recent to jack up prices and point the finger at AI.
The price hikes are "basic eco …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Tim Cook recently said price increases were “unavoidable” and described the company’s pricing as “unsustainable.” The 16-inch MacBook Pro saw its price go up by $300. The 11-inch iPad Air went from $599 to $749. Even the HomePod Mini got a $30 bump to $129. Cook squarely placed the blame at the feet of the AI industry, which is not surprising. RAMageddon has already come for your desktop PCs and gaming consoles. The Xbox has seen its price climb nearly 25 percent depending on the model, and Nothing even canceled an entire phone launch. Apple is just the most recent to jack up prices and point the finger at AI.The price hikes are “basic economics,” says Tim Derdenger, associate professor of marketing and strategy at Carnegie Mellon University’s Tepper School of Business. As the tech industry has raced to win the AI war, “the price of RAM has skyrocketed because the memory manufacturers have reallocated their production lines to produce new HBM memory for AI data centers and away from consumer DDR5.” And when the cost of components goes up, companies tend to pass those costs on to consumers.But this isn’t some fluke, or temporary supply chain problem. Companies are choosing data center clients over ordinary buyers because “the same chip earns far more inside an AI server than inside a consumer device,” according to Srikanth Jagabathula, professor of technology, operations, and statistics at the NYU Stern School of Business. Regardless of whether people are clamoring for more AI, and more AI data centers, or not.Companies like OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft have thrown around unprecedented amounts of money, outbidding companies like Apple for RAM and storage, creating what even Sam Altman has admitted is a bubble. This imbalance has led to record earnings for companies like Micron, which manufactures memory chips. “This shortage is not temporary and might extend into the next few years … And because the increase is lasting rather than temporary, simply absorbing the cost is not a sustainable strategy,” Jagabathula says.But Apple has posted record earnings for at least four quarters in a row, and its margins on hardware sales are much higher than the industry standard. Its markups are estimated to be between 30 and 40 percent, depending on the product. TechInsights and The Wall Street Journal estimate that it’s even higher on the iPhone 17 Pro, perhaps as much as 47 percent. According to TheStreet, margins on smartphones are typically between 15 and 25 percent. Data on laptop margins is harder to come by, but estimates put it between 10 percent and 25 percent for most of the industry.Apple is actually among the last of the major tech companies to raise its prices. But why are customers being asked to foot the bill when Apple seems well-positioned to absorb these costs?Ari Lightman, professor of digital media and marketing at Carnegie Mellon University’s Heinz College, described it as “spot on” to say it’s hard to square Apple’s public financial statements and Tim Cook’s description of its pricing as unsustainable. He said raising prices was “without a doubt” about appeasing shareholders who demand constant growth.Lightman points to Apple’s lagging behind in the AI race, the uncertainty around installing a new CEO in John Ternus, and the lack of a hit new product category as putting pressure on the company.“There’s a lot of things that investors can really beat them up on,” he said, and “if they’re going to be selling the stock and promoting the stock to large institutional investors … in terms of being one of the most valuable companies, then they have to tell a really good story.” And that story is one of huge margins and profits even in the face of rising costs and AI-driven supply constraints.The AI boom is touching almost every facet of our lives, but this week, it came particularly hard for our wallets with the announcement of another round of price hikes for the Xbox, and even the Arduino got caught up in the memory crunch. I spent hours talking to marketing and business experts, exchanging emails and phone calls, and no one could give me a satisfying answer to why the price of popping up more data centers should be consumers’ costs to bear.
items.author: Terrence O’Brien
items.titleZh: 为什么苹果要我为大科技公司的AI痴迷支付更多费用？
items.descriptionZh:
苹果公司近期宣布全线产品涨价，CEO蒂姆·库克直言涨价“不可避免”，并称当前的定价模式“不可持续”。16英寸MacBook Pro价格上涨300美元，11英寸iPad Air从599美元涨至749美元，甚至HomePod Mini也上调了30美元至129美元。库克将矛头直指AI产业，这并不令人意外——一场“内存涨价风暴”早已席卷PC和游戏主机市场：Xbox部分型号价格涨幅接近25%，Nothing甚至直接取消了一款手机的发布。苹果只是最新一家借AI之名上调价格的公司。

卡内基梅隆大学泰珀商学院营销与战略副教授蒂姆·德登格指出，这本质上是“基本经济学”问题：随着科技行业竞相投入AI战争，“内存价格暴涨，因为内存制造商将生产线从消费级DDR5转向为AI数据中心生产新型HBM内存”。当组件成本上升时，企业自然会将成本转嫁给消费者。

但这并非偶然或临时供应链问题。纽约大学斯特恩商学院技术、运营与统计学教授斯里坎特·贾加巴图拉解释，企业之所以优先选择数据中心客户而非普通买家，是因为“同一块芯片在AI服务器中赚取的利润远高于消费设备”。无论公众是否渴望更多AI和AI数据中心，这种趋势已然形成。OpenAI、谷歌、微软等公司投入空前资金，在与苹果等企业竞购内存和存储时抬高价格，就连Sam Altman也承认这已形成泡沫。这种失衡导致美光等内存芯片制造商创下历史性营收纪录。贾加巴图拉警告：“这种短缺并非暂时，可能持续数年……由于涨价是长期而非短暂现象，单纯消化成本并非可持续策略。”

然而，苹果已连续至少四个季度创下营收纪录，其硬件销售利润率远高于行业平均水平。据估算，苹果硬件加价率在30%至40%之间（因产品而异）。TechInsights和《华尔街日报》估计，iPhone 17 Pro的加价率甚至可能高达47%。而根据TheStreet数据，智能手机行业利润率通常为15%至25%，笔记本电脑利润率估计在10%至25%之间。

苹果实际上是大型科技公司中最晚涨价的之一。但既然苹果完全有能力消化这些成本，为何要让消费者买单？卡内基梅隆大学海因茨学院数字媒体与营销教授阿里·莱特曼认为，将苹果公开财报与库克“定价不可持续”的说法联系起来“非常准确”。他表示，涨价“毫无疑问”是为了安抚要求持续增长的股东。莱特曼指出，苹果在AI竞赛中落后、新CEO人选（约翰·特努斯）的不确定性，以及缺乏突破性新产品类别，都对公司形成压力。“投资者有很多理由指责他们，”莱特曼说，“如果要向大型机构投资者推销股票……作为最有价值的公司之一，他们必须讲好一个故事。”这个故事就是：即使在成本上涨和AI驱动的供应限制下，仍能维持高额利润。

AI热潮几乎触及我们生活的方方面面，但本周，它尤其沉重地打击了我们的钱包——Xbox宣布新一轮涨价，甚至连Arduino单片机也被卷入内存短缺。我花了数小时与营销和商业专家交流、收发邮件和通话，但没有一个人能给出令人满意的答案：为什么不断建设更多数据中心的成本，应该由消费者来承担？
